seoTitle: How to Create Two Columns of Text in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to create two columns of text in Canva for newsletters, brochures, and reports that are easier to read and better organized.
intro: A two-column layout helps organize longer blocks of text into a format that's easier to scan, which is why it's common in newsletters, brochures, and reports. Canva doesn't have a single 'columns' button, but you can build the effect quickly with separate text boxes. This guide shows how.

steps.heading: Add your first text box
steps.body: Create a text box and type or paste in the content for your first column.

steps.heading: Resize it to column width
steps.body: Narrow the text box so it takes up roughly half the page width, leaving room for a second column.

steps.heading: Duplicate the text box
steps.body: Copy the first text box and place the duplicate beside it to hold your second column of content.

steps.heading: Align both columns evenly
steps.body: Use Canva's alignment guides to make sure the columns line up at the top and have even spacing between them.

steps.heading: Adjust font size for readability
steps.body: Fine-tune text size so both columns are easy to read without overflowing their boxes.

faq.question: Does Canva have a built-in column tool?
faq.answer: Not directly — columns are created by placing and aligning separate text boxes side by side.

faq.question: Can I use a template that already has two columns?
faq.answer: Yes, many newsletter and brochure templates already include a two-column layout you can edit.

faq.question: How do I keep both columns the same length?
faq.answer: Adjust font size or add/remove content so the total text height matches between the two boxes.

faq.question: Can I add a third column the same way?
faq.answer: Yes, the same duplicate-and-align technique works for adding a third or fourth column.
